target.title: Fix: Guard superseded PR closes against unsafe replacements
target.body:
﻿Fixes #197

## Summary
- Adds apply-time validation for PR-to-PR duplicate/superseded close targets.
- Refuses to close a PR against a linked replacement that is closed unmerged, unreadable, missing positive real behavior proof, F-rated, draft, not cleanly mergeable, or already has its own high-confidence close report.
- Keeps the close path open for merged replacements or open replacements that are cleanly mergeable and proof-positive.
- Protects the same-author issue/PR pair preflight so the issue side does not close if the PR side points at an unsafe replacement.
- Updates the review prompt and README with the safe canonical PR rule.

## Why
ClawSweeper could previously close a PR as duplicate/superseded by another PR even when the replacement was itself closed unmerged or otherwise unsafe. That can hide useful contributor work behind a canonical target that will never land.

## Real behavior proof
Head tested: `1bcf6fcc76`.

The changed close path was exercised through the real `apply-decisions` dry-run path using controlled GitHub fixtures. The proof-positive replacement case still closes, while the no-proof replacement case stays open.

Relevant terminal output from the dry-run regression run:

```text
[apply] ... would close #332 ... counts={"review_comment_synced":1,"closed":1}
[apply] ... finished apply ... counts={"review_comment_synced":1,"kept_open":1}
? apply-decisions promotes PRs superseded by linked pull requests
? apply-decisions does not promote PRs superseded by no-proof linked pull requests
? apply-decisions does not promote PRs superseded by unsafe linked pull requests
? apply-decisions does not promote PRs superseded by F-rated linked pull requests
? apply-decisions does not promote PRs superseded by section-only unsafe linked reports
? apply-decisions promotes PRs when live proof labels supersede stale linked reports
? apply-decisions does not promote PRs when live labels supersede stale proof reports
? tests 22
? pass 22
? fail 0
```

That confirms the specific ClawSweeper review concern: an open, clean canonical replacement PR with no positive proof no longer causes the stale source PR to close.

## Validation
- `pnpm run format`
- `pnpm run build:all`
- `pnpm run format:check`
- `pnpm run lint`
- `node --test --test-name-pattern "no-proof linked|same-author pair close when PR supersession is unsafe|unrelated unsafe|non-clean linked|close comment|section-only unsafe|live labels supersede stale proof|unsafe linked|closed unmerged|bare cluster ref|cannot be read|already proposed for close|skipped close proposal|F-rated|live proof labels|unknown-mergeability|superseded by linked pull requests" test/clawsweeper.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --uncommitted` via the codex-review helper: clean, no accepted/actionable findings

`pnpm run check` also got through active-surface checks, limits, build, lint, and most unit tests, but still hits the existing Windows-local failures unrelated to this change: `/usr/bin/git` is missing for one subprocess test, and a Unix file-mode assertion differs on Windows.
target.author: brokemac79
